LC2H-12V: Bethiah Adams was born in 1661 in Braintree, Massachusetts, where she also lived out her days. She died there in 1698, at about thirty-seven years of age.
LYFP-8HY: Johann Jacob Grimm Sr. was born in 1647 in Körborn, in the Rhineland-Palatinate region of Germany. He made his home in Pfeffelbach, in the Sankt Wendel district, before his death in 1706 in the Zweibrücken area of his native Rhineland-Palatinate.
PZTD-JWC: Colonel John Evans Morgan was born in 1642 in Nantmel, Radnorshire, Wales, where he long resided. He worked as a broadweaver, recorded in 1698 at Uley in Gloucestershire, England. Eventually he crossed the Atlantic, and he died in 1703 in Radnor Township, Chester County, Pennsylvania.
KFTN-ZP6: Dorothea Poyntz lived from 1632 to 1705. She died in Glooston, Leicestershire, England.
KLFD-X85: Nicholas Ware Garrett was born in 1765 in Fairfax Parish, Virginia, during the years of British Colonial America. He appeared in the Fairfax County census of 1782, and in January 1792 he acquired 250 acres of land along Warrior Creek. By 1800 he had settled in Laurens County, South Carolina, where he remained for the rest of his life, with later residence noted at Fountain Inn in Greenville County. He died in Laurens in 1846 at about the age of eighty.
9JQL-2ZG: Elizabeth Goring was born around 1612, with records pointing to Norwich, Norfolk, and to Brereton cum Smethwick in Cheshire, England. She lived for a time at Hurstpierpoint in Sussex. She died in 1687 in Bristol, Gloucestershire.
LZDH-Z2X: Johannes van Gelder was born in 1602 in the Netherlands. Little more is recorded of his life.
LBFX-JZD: Marie Briault was born in 1645 in Rouen, France. As one of the Filles du Roi, the young women sent by the French crown to help settle New France, she departed France and arrived at Québec on 3 July 1668. She made her life in the new colony and died in 1699 in Montreal.
K29F-HLH: John Webb was born in 1655 in Braintree, in the Massachusetts Bay Colony. He died in 1727 in Boston, having spent his life in colonial Massachusetts.
LHZD-3WY: George Chisolm Sr. was born in 1772 in Charleston, South Carolina, then part of British Colonial America. His name appears in Charleston court records repeatedly between 1800 and 1835. He died in his native Charleston in 1835.
K4BF-TF1: Friedrich Jacob Fickeisen was baptized on 9 March 1776 in Kusel, in the Bavarian Palatinate of Germany, where he also resided. He lived until 1831.
LH8F-MG8: Mercy Robinson was born in 1647 in Barnstable, in the Plymouth Colony. She is named in connection with the will and testament of Myles Standish, a noted figure of the colony. She died in 1688 in Falmouth, Barnstable County.
LZBP-CX1: Susanna Denslow was born in 1646 in Windsor, Connecticut, where she also lived out her life. She died in Windsor in 1698.
L87D-JX8: Hester Van Gelder was born in January 1662 in New York City, and she also died there in 1729. Her resting place is recorded in a Find A Grave memorial.
LHDM-56D: Captain Richard Barnes was born in 1680 in Westmoreland, Virginia, during the colonial era. He served in the military and later moved south, dying in 1759 in Chowan County, North Carolina, where his estate passed through probate that same year.
KC66-Z98: Juliana Dorothea Scholler was born in 1725 in the Rhineland-Palatinate region of Germany. She lived until 1791.
LWFC-5MJ: Anna Catherina Emmerich was born in 1668 and made her home in Kusel, in Bavaria, Germany.
LZLC-6GL: Jacques Paviot was born in 1648 and served as a soldier, arriving in New France in August 1665 as a member of the Compagnie de Contrecoeur in the Carignan regiment. His life was a short one; he died in 1674 at Contrecoeur, in the Verchères region of Quebec.
L2GM-NNM: Thomas Atkinson was born in England around 1620. In July 1635 he sailed from England to Virginia aboard the ship Alice, joining the colonial settlement there. He died in 1666 in Surry, Virginia.
LBXJ-K2R: Andrew Lamont Young was born in 1644 in Toward, Argyll, Scotland. In June 1646 his mother, Mary Lamont, changed the family surname out of fear of clan retaliation, and the family resettled in Ireland. He lived until 1722 and died in Antrim, County Antrim, Ireland.
LCZL-7DD: Elizabeth Wilson was born in 1630 in Chirton, Wiltshire, England. She crossed the Atlantic and died in 1734, at well over a century of age, in Archdale, Dorchester County, South Carolina.
M4DF-7X4: George Bradshaw was born around 1580, with records placing his birth in London and, alternatively, in Leicestershire, England. He emigrated to the American colonies and died in 1640 in Northumberland, Virginia.
LDHJ-NWG: Pierre Joufriau was born in 1638 in Fontenay-le-Comte, in the Vendée region of France. He settled in Montreal, New France, where he worked as a mason and lived as a habitant. He died there in 1704.
9CXK-ZKS: Abigail Baxter was born in 1634 in Roxbury, in the Massachusetts Bay Colony. She died in 1692 in Braintree, having lived her life in colonial Massachusetts.
L2CC-H4R: Anna Weissin was born in 1695 and baptized that 20 October in Sissach, in the Basel region of Switzerland, where she also resided. In 1736 she made the voyage to America, arriving in South Carolina. She died in 1769 in Rowan County, North Carolina.
L65S-S3P: John Garnett II was born in 1648 in Warwick, Virginia, during the colonial period. He was an active landholder, acquiring property on the south side of Garden Creek in 1676 and a further 260 acres there in 1683, while the 1704 Quit Rent Rolls list him as owning 250 acres. In 1692 he was party to a land transaction involving a tract known as "Buttons Range." He died in 1703 in Gloucester, Virginia.
LX31-FNB: William Slade was born around 1630 in England, with roots in Melksham, Wiltshire. He emigrated to Anne Arundel County, Maryland, in 1649, and over the following decades he built up extensive landholdings, including tracts known as "Wolf Neck," "Slade's Hope," "Curtis' Neck," and "Slade's Addition." He died in 1676 in Anne Arundel County.
LZ65-HLN: Sarah Learned was born in 1611 in Bermondsey, Surrey, England. She crossed the Atlantic to the Massachusetts Bay Colony and died in 1652 in Barnstable.
